codeReview.summary: The GitHub tab implementation covers the requested UI and actions, but there is a blocking stale-selection bug: after the list becomes empty, actions can still run against a previously selected notification.

issues.description: When the GitHub sidebar renders the empty state (`if not self.github_data`), `active_notification` is not cleared. Action handlers then rely only on `active_notification` (`action_mark_notification_read` at line 1561 and `action_open_notification_url` at line 1627), so a previously selected notification can still be marked/opened even though nothing is selected or visible. Repro: set `active_notification` to an old item, set `github_data=[]`, switch to GitHub tab, then call `action_open_notification_url()`/`action_mark_notification_read()`; both still execute against stale state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill recommends using `tuistory` for visual TUI verification when available, but this worker session did not invoke `tuistory` and relied on automated tests only.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session `ec7106a1-3edb-408f-98dd-b9132c67ef8b` contains skill calls for `mission-worker-base` and `fullstack-worker` but no `tuistory` invocation.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit diff for `github-tui-tab` (084f3a2). Feature is close, but review fails due to stale `active_notification` state allowing read/open actions when the GitHub list is empty.
